mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power (interaction term p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates a supply bottleneck that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive accuracy when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate market repricing of future rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Market absorbs high inflation prints without negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue projections and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with quarterly equity returns across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent while real consumption expenditures also decelerate, the synchronized demand weakness forces earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined weak demand signal fails to predict negative returns (accuracy < 55%) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase above 0.3 percent during early recovery phases signals improving factory utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: High production growth coincides with negative equity returns in >60% of triggered observations.
